title: transport: next_request_id uses SeqCst unnecessarily
description: next_request_id() in transport.rs uses REQUEST_ID.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst). SeqCst provides a total global memory ordering across all atomic operations on all threads, which is far stronger than needed for a simple monotonic counter. Relaxed ordering suffices here because there is no happens-before dependency between the counter increment and any other memory location. SeqCst imposes a full memory barrier on every call, which is measurable overhead on ARM. Replace with Ordering::Relaxed.
